subject_output:
# New Recipe: `cooked_fish`

Following the schema in `gdd/content/recipes.md` and matching the envelope/format conventions used by the three existing recipe YAML files (`wooden_axe.yaml`, `fiber_rope.yaml`, `signal_pyre.yaml`).

**File:** `benchmark/games/survival/content/recipes/cooked_fish.yaml`

```yaml
spec: game-design.md
spec_version: 0.2.0-alpha
file_type: content-entity
id: cooked_fish
status: draft
implemented_in: ["src/driftwood/content/recipes/cooked_fish.py"]
name: "Cooked Fish"
tier: 2
inputs:
  - { item: raw_fish, quantity: 1 }
output:
  item: cooked_fish
  quantity: 1
  tag: food
  hunger_restore_value: 6
station_required: campfire
tool_required: wooden_axe
in_game_minutes_to_craft: 30
```

## How this maps to the schema and the task

| Field | Value | Source / reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `cooked_fish` | Matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`; matches filename stem per schema prose. |
| `name` | `"Cooked Fish"` | Human-readable UI string. |
| `tier` | `2` | Task specifies tier-2 (wooden axe as the gating tool tier); within schema's `[0, 4]` range. |
| `inputs` | `[{ item: raw_fish, quantity: 1 }]` | Task: "turns 1 raw_fish into 1 cooked_fish". |
| `output.item` | `cooked_fish` | The produced item. |
| `output.quantity` | `1` | Task: produces 1 cooked_fish per craft. |
| `output.tag` | `food` | One of the enum values; the schema prose notes `verbs.eat` routes on `tag: food` (see `gdd/mechanics.md` `verbs.eat.target_schema.filter: tag_equals_food`). |
| `output.hunger_restore_value` | `6` | Task: "refills 6 hunger when eaten". Used by `rules.eat_resolution`'s `increase_resource ... by: {target.hunger_restore_value}` step. Within schema bound `minimum: 0`; well within the hunger clamp `[0, 12]`. |
| `station_required` | `campfire` | Task: "at a campfire". One of the four station kinds in `entities.crafting_station.properties.kind` enum. |
| `tool_required` | `wooden_axe` | Tier-2 gate per the schema prose ("Tier governs which prior tool/station is implied as a prerequisite"). The `wooden_axe` recipe itself defines this tool. |
| `in_game_minutes_to_craft` | `30` | Task: "about 30 in-game minutes". Matches the `fiber_rope` recipe's fast-craft precedent (also 30). |

## Anti-drift / consistency checks (no code changes required)

- **Schema conformance.** All required fields present; `id` matches the pattern; `tier` in `[0,4]`; `output.tag` in the enum; `hunger_restore_value` ≥ 0.
- **Integer quantities (`integer_resource_quantities` invariant).** All numeric fields are integers (`1`, `2`, `6`, `30`) — no floats introduced.
- **Recipe data immutability (`recipe_data_immutable_at_runtime` invariant).** New recipe is added as a single new YAML file in `content/recipes/`; no runtime mutation, no edits to existing recipes.
- **Single-file scope.** Task constraint honored — only one new file in `content/recipes/`; no edits to `gdd/`, no edits to existing recipe YAMLs, no other source files touched.
- **Recipe-count target.** `gdd/content/recipes.md` declares `count_target: 12`. Existing on-disk recipes are three (`wooden_axe`, `fiber_rope`, `signal_pyre`); adding `cooked_fish` brings the count to four, still within the target window (the other eight recipes are unauthored in this benchmark snapshot and outside this task's scope).
- **Balance touchpoint.** `cooked_fish` does not consume wood/fiber/stone/flint, so it does not contribute to `balance_targets.pyre_input_total`; it is a food-loop recipe, orthogonal to the pyre material budget. No balance-target regression.

# New Recipe: `cooked_fish`

Following the schema in `gdd/content/recipes.md` and matching the envelope/format conventions used by the three existing recipe YAML files (`wooden_axe.yaml`, `fiber_rope.yaml`, `signal_pyre.yaml`).

**File:** `benchmark/games/survival/content/recipes/cooked_fish.yaml`

```yaml

id: cooked_fish

name: "Cooked Fish"
tier: 2
inputs:
  - { item: raw_fish, quantity: 1 }
output:
  item: cooked_fish
  quantity: 1
  tag: food
  hunger_restore_value: 6
station_required: campfire
tool_required: wooden_axe
in_game_minutes_to_craft: 30
```

How this maps to the schema and the task

| Field | Value | Source / reasoning |
|---|---|---|
| `id` | `cooked_fish` | Matches `^[a-z][a-z0-9_]*$`; matches filename stem per schema prose. |
| `name` | `"Cooked Fish"` | Human-readable UI string. |
| `tier` | `2` | Task specifies tier-2 (wooden axe as the gating tool tier); within schema's `[0, 4]` range. |
| `inputs` | `[{ item: raw_fish, quantity: 1 }]` | Task: "turns 1 raw_fish into 1 cooked_fish". |
| `output.item` | `cooked_fish` | The produced item. |
| `output.quantity` | `1` | Task: produces 1 cooked_fish per craft. |
| `output.tag` | `food` | One of the enum values; the schema prose notes `eat` routes on `tag: food` (see `gdd/mechanics.md` `eat.target_schema.filter: tag_equals_food`). |
| `output.hunger_restore_value` | `6` | Task: "refills 6 hunger when eaten". Used by `eat_resolution`'s `increase_resource ... by: hunger_restore_value` step. Within schema bound `minimum: 0`; well within the hunger clamp `[0, 12]`. |
| `station_required` | `campfire` | Task: "at a campfire". One of the four station kinds in `crafting_station.properties.kind` enum. |
| `tool_required` | `wooden_axe` | Tier-2 gate per the schema prose ("Tier governs which prior tool/station is implied as a prerequisite"). The `wooden_axe` recipe itself defines this tool. |
| `in_game_minutes_to_craft` | `30` | Task: "about 30 in-game minutes". Matches the `fiber_rope` recipe's fast-craft precedent (also 30). |

Anti-drift / consistency checks (no code changes required)

- **Schema conformance.** All required fields present; `id` matches the pattern; `tier` in `[0,4]`; `output.tag` in the enum; `hunger_restore_value` ≥ 0.
- **Integer quantities (`integer_resource_quantities` invariant).** All numeric fields are integers (`1`, `2`, `6`, `30`) — no floats introduced.
- **Recipe data immutability (`recipe_data_immutable_at_runtime` invariant).** New recipe is added as a single new YAML file in `content/recipes/`; no runtime mutation, no edits to existing recipes.
- **Single-file scope.** Task constraint honored — only one new file in `content/recipes/`; no edits to `gdd/`, no edits to existing recipe YAMLs, no other source files touched.
- **Recipe-count target.** `gdd/content/recipes.md` declares `count_target: 12`. Existing on-disk recipes are three (`wooden_axe`, `fiber_rope`, `signal_pyre`); adding `cooked_fish` brings the count to four, still within the target window (the other eight recipes are unauthored in this benchmark snapshot and outside this task's scope).
- **Balance touchpoint.** `cooked_fish` does not consume wood/fiber/stone/flint, so it does not contribute to `pyre_input_total`; it is a food-loop recipe, orthogonal to the pyre material budget. No balance-target regression.

intent_score.rationale: The output perfectly implements the task brief while remaining strictly consistent with the design brief. It correctly identifies the tier-2 requirement, the campfire station, the specific hunger restoration value of 6, and the 30-minute craft time. All design choices, such as the tool requirement and item tags, are defensible against the provided game mechanics.
